key_insight: A physics-informed conditional DDPM can forecast phase-field fracture evolution with built-in uncertainty maps, while staying stable over long autoregressive rollouts.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster-than-FEM crack growth prediction with localized confidence estimates, helping engineers assess branching, tip motion, and failure risk in near-real time.
method: Conditioned diffusion model on rolling damage history plus kinematic proxies: phase-field velocity and gradient magnitude.
Generated full-field spatiotemporal fracture forecasts for multiple loading regimes and energy decomposition settings.
Used ensemble variance from stochastic re-sampling for uncertainty quantification without changing the trained model.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 0.12 px mean crack-tip error, Dice = 0.929 ± 0.010 over 50-step closed-loop rollout, and 90% precision for high-error detection from high-σ pixels.
watch_item: Inference is still costly for deployment, at about 3.6 s per step on an H100 GPU, so real-time use may require further acceleration or surrogate optimization.
paper_title: Uncertainty-Aware Crack Growth Forecasting via Conditional Denoising Diffusion Models for Phase-Field Fracture
